en: turns out to be pretty accurate.
In a 2014 experiment, scientists placed a food reward
in a tube partially filled with water
and watched as crow after crow dropped stone after stone in
ko: 까마귀들이 하나씩 돌을 떨어뜨리는 것을 관찰했습니다

en: until the food was in beak-snatching distance.
ko: 먹이가 부리로 잡을 수 있는 거리에 올 때까지요.

en: This is just one of many fascinating displays
of intelligence from corvids,
ko: 많은 놀라운 지능의 사례 중 하나일 뿐입니다.

en: the bold, brainy family of songbirds
that includes crows, ravens, jays, and magpies.
To start, corvids are strategic when it comes to their food.
ko: 먼저, 까마귀과 새들은 먹이에 대해 전략적입니다.

en: They ransack trash bins and snatch bites
ko: 쓰레기통을 뒤지고 다른 새들의 부리 아래에서

en: from under other birds' beaks.
They also hoard and cache their food.
A single Clark's nutcracker, for instance,
is thought to scatter some 30,000 pine nuts
across more than 2,000 cache sites,
ko: 2,000개 이상의 저장 장소에 흩어놓는 것으로 알려져 있으며,

en: which it can recover more than nine months later.
And in one experiment, scrub jays,
given waxworms and peanuts,
cached and retrieved the perishable worms first
ko: 상하기 쉬운 벌레를 먼저 저장하고 회수한 후

en: and left the peanuts for later.
It seemed the jays not only recalled where they buried things,
but also what they buried and when.
ko: 무엇을, 언제 묻었는지도 기억하는 것 같았습니다.
